chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, Bench at Sukkur C. P. No. D – 2277 of 2017 (Abdul Wahab & others V/S P.O. Sindh & Others) Fresh Case 1.For orders on CMA 2028/2018 2.For hearing of Main Case. 3.For orders on CMA 2029/2018 Before: Mr. Justice Muhammad Junaid Ghaffar Mr. Justice Zulfiqar Ali Sangi Date of Hearing: 22-02-2022 Mr. Qurban Ali Malano, Advocate for the Petitioners. O R D E R Granted. (2&3) Through this Constitutional Petition, the Petitioners have impugned the Verification Letter dated 27.07.2015 as being void and illegal with further prayer for release of their salaries. Learned Counsel for the Petitioners has argued that the salaries are not being paid to the Petitioners despite their valid appointments in the year 2012, which according to him also stands verified; hence this Petition. However, we have confronted the Petitioners’ Counsel with Letter dated 27.07.2015, issued by the Local government Department, wherein it has been stated that only 137 employees of Municipal Committee Moro have been issued genuine and verified appointment orders; whereas, remaining 107 employees including present Petitioners appointments are fake and fabricated, and in response, learned Counsel has not been able to convince us with any plausible explanation. Since it is a dispute which is factual in nature, whereby, appointment orders by itself have been found to be fake, notwithstanding that subsequently even salaries were paid to the Petitioners, we in this constitutional petition cannot adjudicate the same. Hence, this Petition being not maintainable is dismissed in limine with pending application. J U D G E J U D G E Ahmad
